articles.title: Prediction markets may be getting a rule book
articles.summary: Regulators are apparently working on formal rules to govern how prediction markets operate in the US. Clear regulations would legitimize the space and attract institutional money, but they could also kill the Wild West innovation that made these markets interesting in the first place.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Are Costing States Millions In Tax Revenue
articles.summary: States are losing millions in tax revenue as bettors flock to prediction markets that operate in legal gray areas or offshore platforms. This revenue drain will likely accelerate regulatory crackdowns and push lawmakers to either ban these markets outright or create taxable frameworks that bring the money back home.

articles.title: Kalshi Taps Brazil’s XP to Take Prediction Markets Global
articles.summary: Kalshi partnered with Brazil's XP, one of Latin America's largest investment platforms, to expand prediction markets internationally. The move signals serious institutional backing for bringing regulated prediction markets to global retail investors, potentially unlocking massive liquidity from XP's 3.7 million clients.

articles.title: How Kalshi, Polymarket bets on mortgage rates are being watched by lenders
articles.summary: Mortgage lenders are now monitoring prediction market bets on Kalshi and Polymarket to gauge where interest rates are heading. This marks a major shift where traditional financial institutions are treating retail betting markets as legitimate sources of forward-looking economic intelligence.

articles.title: My Prediction: Prediction Market ETFs Will Be a Huge Disappointment for Long-Term Investors
articles.summary: Prediction market ETFs will likely underperform traditional investments because they're structured to extract profits from volatile, short-term betting markets rather than generate steady long-term returns. The mismatch between what retail investors expect from ETFs and what prediction markets actually deliver could kill mainstream adoption before it really starts.

articles.title: Kalshi Teams Up With Brazil’s XP for First International Push
articles.summary: Kalshi is partnering with Brazilian financial giant XP to expand prediction markets internationally for the first time. This marks a huge step toward global adoption of regulated event betting, potentially opening up massive new liquidity pools and trading opportunities beyond US markets.

articles.title: Trump is the GOAT in swaying GOP races — and prediction markets say he’ll decide Texas this way
articles.summary: Trump's endorsement power in Republican primaries remains unmatched, and prediction markets are betting he'll throw his weight behind a specific candidate in an upcoming Texas race. His track record of moving GOP primary odds by 20-30 points overnight makes any Trump endorsement signal pure gold for bettors watching Texas markets.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Are Booming, but I'd Rather Bet on These 3 AI Stocks
articles.summary: Some finance writer is hyping AI stocks over prediction markets, claiming the sector offers better returns than betting on political outcomes or sports. This matters because if institutional money starts viewing AI equities as the superior speculation play, it could drain liquidity from prediction markets just as they're hitting mainstream adoption.

articles.title: Polymarket Is Fun, but Here's Where You Should Really Put Your Money in AI
articles.summary: Polymarket dominates the prediction market headlines, but the real AI investment opportunities lie in the infrastructure powering these platforms and the data they generate. Smart money knows that betting on the picks and shovels beats betting on individual outcomes, especially when those tools are reshaping how we quantify uncertainty across every industry.

articles.title: Who Will Exit The Trump White House In 2026? Prediction Market Is Betting On This Person
articles.summary: Prediction markets are already placing bets on which Trump administration officials will be the first to leave in 2026, with one particular figure emerging as the favorite to exit early. The action signals that bettors expect significant White House turnover during Trump's second term, creating opportunities for those who can correctly predict the administration's internal dynamics.

articles.title: The Bettors on Polymarket Don't Think Bitcoin Will Hit $150,000 in March. Here's What I Think.
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are betting against Bitcoin reaching $150,000 by March, suggesting the crypto crowd thinks the current rally has limits. This reflects how prediction markets now serve as real-time sentiment gauges for volatile assets, giving us clearer reads on where smart money actually stands versus the hype machine.

articles.title: Insider Trading Is Going to Get People Killed
articles.summary: Corporate executives are trading on material nonpublic information about deadly product defects, safety failures, and health crises before the news breaks. When pharmaceutical CEOs dump stock days before announcing trial failures or auto executives short their shares ahead of recall announcements, prediction markets become the only reliable way to spot these patterns and price in real risk.
